summary: Dashboard displays authenticated UI with navigation sidebar and main content area. Shows estimated bill widget, free plan usage cards, and empty indexes state. However, this violates brand palette consistency rules as authenticated surfaces use generic dark/neutral colors instead of the required diner brand palette.

violations.description: Logged-in dashboard route uses generic dark palette (#2c3e50-style dark sidebar, neutral grays) while public routes use distinct diner brand treatment (teal #9fd8d2, cream #fff8ea, etc.). This creates brand discontinuity after signup/login.
Authenticated dashboard uses neutral gray UI defaults instead of required diner palette surfaces. Primary canvas should use teal #9fd8d2 or cream #fff8ea variants, not generic dark backgrounds.
Public legal/marketing palette and signed-in app chrome are not visually separated as required - they should share coherent brand palette rather than diverge into different visual systems.

actions: Apply diner brand palette to authenticated dashboard surfaces
Replace dark sidebar navigation with cream #fff8ea or teal #9fd8d2 brand colors
Update main content area background to use brand palette instead of white/gray
Ensure consistent brand visual language between public and authenticated routes
